rawContent: Go-live Monday 11 March 6am UTC. Checklist: DNS cutover, health checks passing on all 12 services, DB replication lag < 1s, rollback plan rehearsed. Oncall: Marcus (backend), Priya (infra). War room: #migration-golive Slack channel. Stakeholders notified.
structuredContent:
# Go-Live — Monday March 11, 2026 @ 06:00 UTC

## Pre-Launch Checklist

### Infrastructure
- [ ] DNS cutover prepared and tested
- [ ] Health checks passing on all **12 microservices**
- [ ] DB replication lag confirmed < 1 second
- [ ] Load balancer routing verified

### Rollback Plan
- [ ] Rollback procedure rehearsed (< 15 min target)
- [ ] Feature flags configured for instant rollback
- [ ] Old monolith kept warm for 48h post-cutover

## On-Call Rotation
| Role | Person | Contact |
|------|--------|---------|
| Backend | Marcus | Slack @marcus |
| Infrastructure | Priya | PagerDuty |

## Communication
- **War room**: `#migration-golive` (Slack)
- All stakeholders notified via email
- Status page updates every 30min during window

## ✅ Decisions
- Go/No-Go call at **05:45 UTC** with CTO

meta.document_reason: Operational reference for the migration window and team coordination

rawContent: Went well: service mesh setup smoother than expected, Priya did great work on terraform modules. Needs improvement: too many context switches between migration and feature work. Action: dedicated migration days Mon/Tue. Blocker resolved: Redis cluster auth issue fixed by rotating certs.
structuredContent:
# Team Retrospective — Sprint 4

## ✅ What Went Well
- **Service mesh setup** completed ahead of schedule (Istio config cleaner than anticipated)
- **Priya's Terraform modules** — reusable, well-documented, saved ~2 days of work
- Zero regressions in auth-service after refactoring

## 🔧 Needs Improvement
- **Context switching** between migration tasks and ongoing feature requests is hurting focus
  - Engineers are pulled into bug fixes mid-migration

## 🔴 Blockers
- ~~Redis cluster auth issue~~ **RESOLVED** — Fixed by rotating TLS certificates
  - Root cause: expired cert not caught by monitoring (alert rule added)

## Actions
- [ ] Block **Monday + Tuesday** as dedicated migration days (no feature work)
- [ ] PM to shield team from non-critical interruptions this week
- [ ] Add Redis cert expiry alert to runbook

## Metrics — Sprint 4
- Services migrated: 4/12 ✅
- Test coverage: 87% (target: 85%) ✅
- Open migration PRs: 3

rawContent: Decision: use AWS API Gateway v2 over Kong. Reasons: managed service, native IAM auth, lower ops burden. Rate limiting: 1000 req/min per tenant. Custom domain: api.scalr.io. Versioning strategy: URL path /v1/ /v2/. Breaking changes require 90 day deprecation notice.
structuredContent:
# ADR-005 — API Gateway Selection

## Decision
**Chosen: AWS API Gateway v2** (over Kong, Nginx, Traefik)

## Context
Needed an API gateway to handle external routing, auth, and rate limiting for the new microservices architecture.

## Rationale

| Criterion | AWS API GW v2 | Kong |
|-----------|--------------|------|
| Ops burden | Low (managed) | High (self-hosted) |
| IAM auth | Native | Plugin required |
| Cost | Pay-per-request | Fixed infra cost |
| Learning curve | Low for team | Medium |

## Configuration
- **Rate limiting**: 1,000 req/min per tenant
- **Custom domain**: `api.scalr.io`
- **Versioning**: URL path strategy (`/v1/`, `/v2/`)
- **Breaking change policy**: 90-day deprecation notice required

## Consequences
- Vendor lock-in to AWS (accepted — already AWS-native)
- No custom plugins (acceptable for current needs)
- Saves ~1 week of Kong setup and maintenance

## Status: ✅ Approved — 2026-03-05

rawContent: Pentest by SecureOps: 3 findings. HIGH: exposed debug endpoint /admin/debug on staging (fixed). MEDIUM: JWT expiry too long 7 days reduced to 1h plus refresh token. LOW: verbose error messages leaking stack traces. All highs and mediums fixed before go-live. SOC2 Type II audit scheduled April.
structuredContent:
# Security Audit Report — Pre-Migration Pentest

**Conducted by**: SecureOps Ltd
**Date**: March 4, 2026
**Scope**: All 12 microservices, API gateway, infrastructure

## Findings Summary

| Severity | Count | Status |
|----------|-------|--------|
| 🔴 HIGH | 1 | ✅ Fixed |
| 🟡 MEDIUM | 1 | ✅ Fixed |
| 🟢 LOW | 1 | Planned |

## Detailed Findings

### 🔴 HIGH — Debug endpoint exposed
- **Endpoint**: `/admin/debug` accessible on staging environment
- **Risk**: Full request/response logging, env variables visible
- **Fix**: Endpoint removed, firewall rule added for staging
- **Status**: ✅ Fixed (March 4)

### 🟡 MEDIUM — JWT expiry too long
- **Issue**: Access tokens valid for 7 days
- **Risk**: Stolen token usable for extended period
- **Fix**: Reduced to **1 hour** + added refresh token flow
- **Status**: ✅ Fixed (March 5)

### 🟢 LOW — Verbose error messages
- **Issue**: Stack traces returned in production error responses
- **Fix**: Generic error handler with correlation IDs only
- **Status**: Scheduled for Sprint 5

## Compliance
- **SOC2 Type II** audit scheduled: **April 2026**
- All HIGH/MEDIUM findings must be resolved before go-live ✅

## Open Questions
- Will LOW finding block SOC2 audit?

meta.document_title: Security Audit Report — Scalr Pre-Migration Findings

rawContent: Strategy: blue-green migration. Phase 1: dual-write to old and new DB. Phase 2: verify consistency cutover reads. Phase 3: stop writes to old DB verify decommission. Data volume: 2.3TB PostgreSQL. Estimated migration window: 4 hours. Tooling: pglogical for replication. Risk: large blob storage 180GB S3.
structuredContent:
# Database Migration Strategy

## Approach: Blue-Green Migration

Chosen to guarantee **zero-downtime** and immediate rollback capability.

## Migration Phases

### Phase 1 — Dual Write
- Both old and new databases receive all writes simultaneously
- Duration: ~2 weeks of parallel operation
- Goal: build replication lag baseline

### Phase 2 — Read Cutover
- Switch all **read traffic** to new DB
- Monitor for consistency issues (automated diff checks every 15min)
- Old DB still receiving writes as fallback

### Phase 3 — Write Cutover & Decommission
- Stop writes to old DB
- 48h verification period
- Decommission old DB instance

## Technical Details
| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Data volume | 2.3 TB PostgreSQL |
| Replication tool | pglogical |
| Migration window | ~4 hours (Phase 3) |
| Blob storage | 180 GB (S3, separate migration) |

## Risks & Mitigations
- **S3 blob storage (180GB)**: Separate migration plan required — not covered by pglogical
- **Replication lag spikes**: Alert if lag > 5s, pause traffic if > 30s
- **Schema drift**: All migrations frozen 2 weeks before go-live

## TODO
- [ ] S3 blob migration plan (owner: Marcus)
- [ ] Load test dual-write performance impact
- [ ] Document rollback procedure for Phase 3

rawContent: Current monolith: p95 latency 340ms p99 890ms. Target post-migration: p95 under 100ms. Bottlenecks: N+1 queries on user dashboard accounts for 60% of DB load, sync report generation blocking request thread. Memory 4.2GB average RSS on prod pods. Load test max 800 concurrent users before degradation.
structuredContent:
# Performance Baseline — Scalr Monolith

## Current Metrics (Production)

| Metric | Current | Target (post-migration) |
|--------|---------|------------------------|
| p95 latency | **340ms** | < 100ms |
| p99 latency | **890ms** | < 300ms |
| Max concurrent users | **800** | 5,000+ |
| Memory (avg RSS) | **4.2 GB** | < 512 MB/service |

## Bottlenecks Identified

### 🔴 Critical — N+1 Queries (User Dashboard)
- Accounts for **60% of total DB load**
- Each dashboard load triggers 40–80 individual queries
- **Fix**: Implement DataLoader + query batching in user-service

### 🟡 High — Synchronous Report Generation
- Report generation **blocks the request thread** (up to 8 seconds)
- **Fix**: Move to async job queue (SQS + worker), return job ID with polling

### 🟢 Medium — Memory Bloat
- 4.2 GB RSS from caching everything in-process
- **Fix**: Move caches to Redis (already planned)

## Load Test Results
- Degradation starts at **800 concurrent users**
- Error rate > 1% at 950 users
- Complete failure at 1,200 users

## Open Questions
- Will splitting into 12 services eliminate the N+1 problem or just move it?

rawContent: Splitting monolith into: auth-service, user-service, workspace-service, reporting-service, notification-service, billing-service. Communication: gRPC for internal REST for external. Event bus: AWS EventBridge. Each service owns its DB schema. Team assignment per service. Target: fully split by March 15.
structuredContent:
# ADR-001 — Microservices Architecture

## Context
The Scalr monolith has reached scalability limits. p99 latency at 890ms, team velocity declining due to deployment coupling, and infrastructure costs scaling linearly with users.

## Decision
Split into **6 bounded microservices**, each owning its data and deployed independently.

## Services

| Service | Responsibility | Owner |
|---------|---------------|-------|
| auth-service | Authentication, JWT, OAuth2 | Marcus |
| user-service | User profiles, permissions | Sara |
| workspace-service | Notebooks, notes, documents | Dev |
| reporting-service | Async report generation | Priya |
| notification-service | Email, webhooks, push | Alex |
| billing-service | Stripe integration, invoices | Marcus |

## Communication
- **Internal (service-to-service)**: gRPC (low latency, typed contracts)
- **External (client-to-API)**: REST via AWS API Gateway
- **Async events**: AWS EventBridge (audit logs, notifications, billing events)

## Data Ownership
- Each service owns its own DB schema
- No direct cross-service DB queries allowed
- Cross-service data via API or event stream only

## Target Timeline
- All 6 services split and deployed: **March 15, 2026**
- Full traffic cutover: **March 11, 2026** *(go-live)*

## Consequences
- ✅ Independent deployability per service
- ✅ Horizontal scaling per service
- ⚠️ Increased operational complexity (12 deployables)
- ⚠️ Distributed tracing required (OpenTelemetry planned)

## Status: ✅ Approved — 2026-02-20

meta.document_reason: Formal ADR document for engineering wiki and future team reference
